text.title: go-concurrency-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-concurrency-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The caller cannot know whether jobs succeeded. Wire to an error channel or at minimum a structured logger with the job identifier.

failure-mode.description: Loop variable capture (pre-Go 1.22): for _, x := range xs { go func() { use(x) }() }
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: All goroutines capture the same loop variable and read its final value. Force x := x before the go statement, or use Go 1.22+ range semantics.

failure-mode.description: Adding a default: branch to a select that waits on a channel and ctx.Done().
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: default: converts the blocking select into a busy-loop, consuming 100% CPU while waiting.

failure-mode.description: Forgetting close(jobs) after all work is submitted in a worker pool.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Workers wait on the channel forever. Tests hang until go test -timeout kills them. Always defer close(jobs) after the submit loop.

failure-mode.description: Mixing sync.Mutex and channels on the same shared state.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Two concurrency models on one variable creates a category of bugs that neither model's tools can catch. Pick one: mutex for shared state, channels for message passing.
